Soral
Cmblems.

FIG.
9.

SAlterezza
in
persona
nata
povera
civile:

lA
HAHGHTY
BEGGAR.

A
Damsel,
blind,
with
a
losty
Countenance,
in
a
pompous
red
Mantle

adorn’d
with
several
Jewels,
under
which
is
a
poor
Petticoat;
having
a

Peacock
under
her
right
Arm,
holding
up
her
lest,
standing
with
one
Foot

upon
a
Bowl,
and
with
the
other
seems
to
precipitate
herself.

The
red
Garment,
the
Heat
of
Blood
causing
Ambitien;
the
pitiful

Petticoat,
that
the
haughty
at
the
Bottom
have
nothing
worthy
Elleem.

Her
Posture
shews
the
tichlish
Place
she
is
in,
and
like
to
fall
into
Misery.

FIG.
10.
Altimetria
:
TARING
A
HEIGHT
GEOMETRICALLI.

A
voung
Lady
in
a
proper
Posture,
holds
with
both
her
Hands
a

Gcometrical
Quadran,
taking
the
Height
of
a
Tower.

Young,
because
Daughter
of
Geometry,
observing
all
the
Measures

taught
by
it;
all
other
Circumstances
are
specified
in
Geometry
:
the

Instruments,
at
her
Feet,
are
us’d
in
Surveying.

FIC.
11.
Ambitione
:
AMBITION.

A
Virgin
all
in
green,
with
Ivy
Branches,
looking
as
it
lhe
would

leap
over
a
craggy
Rock,
at
the
Top
of
which
are
Scepters
and
Crowns,

attended
by
a
Lion
listing
up
his
Head.

The
Ivy,
denotes
Ambition
alw’ays
climbing
higher
and
higher,
spoiling

the
Walls;
the
Ambitious
sparing
neither
Country,
Religion,
nor

Counsellours,
lo
he
may
become
greater
than
others
The
Lion,
Pride.

FIC.
12.
Amicita
:
FRIENDSHIP.

She
wears
a
plain
white
Robe,
her
left
Shoulder
is
nakcd,
with
a

Garland.
Che
holds
a
Heart
in
her
right
Hand,
delights
to
go
barefoot,

and
grasps
a
wither'd
Elm
incircled
with
a
Vinc.

Her
Livery
is
white,
and
her
Garment
void
of
all
Ornament,
denoting

her
Freeness
without
Artifice;
her
bare
Fect,
her
undergoing
all
Hardships

to
serve
her
Frined.
The
dry
Elm
embrac’d,
shews
that
Friendship
ought

to
appeat
in
Adverfity,
as
vell
as
Prosperity.
